status_detail: Hydrogen mobility network in France: HysetCo developing hydrogen refueling stations supplied by Normand'Hy (200 MW PEM). EUR 50M Air Liquide investment for Seine Axis HRS network. Targeting heavy-duty mobility decarbonization.

data_management.narrative_summary: HysetCo and Air Liquide are developing a hydrogen refueling station network along the Seine Axis in France (Le Havre-Rouen-Paris corridor), supplied with green hydrogen from the Normand'Hy 200 MW PEM electrolysis plant (GA-PR-001). Air Liquide invested EUR 50 million in the hydrogen supply chain for this network. The project targets heavy-duty mobility decarbonization for trucks, buses, and logistics vehicles operating along one of France's busiest transport corridors. This project demonstrates the integrated approach of large-scale electrolysis production linked to distributed offtake demand. Approximately 25% of Normand'Hy's production is allocated to mobility applications.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected. Hydrogen supplied from Normand'Hy 200 MW plant.

offtake.offtaker_name: HysetCo, Seine Axis HRS network (heavy-duty trucks, buses, logistics)
offtake.offtake_agreement_type: Long-term offtake agreement with Air Liquide Normand'Hy

sources.title: Air Liquide invests EUR 50M in hydrogen supply chain for Seine Axis mobility network
sources.author: Air Liquide
